figures.caption: Figure 1. Geographic distribution, genome architecture, and chromosomal fusions in two clades of the Eurytemora 100 affinis species complex. (a) Geographic distribution of two clades (sibling species) of the E. affinis species complex43,45, 101 showing the Europe clade (E. affinis proper; haploid chromosome number = 15, purple) and the Atlantic clade (E. carolleeae; 102 haploid chromosome number = 4, red). Native (circles) and invaded (triangles) locations are shown for populations from each 103 clade. (b) Genome architecture of the two clades, illustrating the dramatic difference in chromosome number (15 versus 4) due 104 to chromosomal fusions in the Atlantic clade29. (c) Schematic representation of chromosomal fusions and associated gene 105 repositioning between the two clades. Fifteen ancestral chromosomes in the Europe clade correspond to four fused 106 chromosomes in the Atlantic clade, with several key ion transporter genes (e.g., NKCC, NHA, and NKA gene families) 107 repositioned near fusion sites29. Centromere positions are indicated by open circles. 108 109
